question_text: Two springs are tested. Spring A extends by 8 cm when a 4 N force is applied. Spring B extends by 4 cm when the same 4 N force is applied. Which spring is stiffer, and explain your reasoning using the concept of spring constant.
correct_answer: Spring B is stiffer. Spring B has a higher spring constant (k = 4 ÷ 0.04 = 100 N/m) compared to Spring A (k = 4 ÷ 0.08 = 50 N/m). A stiffer spring has a larger spring constant and extends less for the same applied force.
explanation: A stiffer spring requires more force to produce the same extension, or produces less extension for the same force. Since Spring B extends less (4 cm vs 8 cm) under the same force, it must be stiffer. We can verify this by calculating spring constants using F = k × e.
